| Query | Coverage |
|---|---|
| SNOMED CT `91302008` | Sepsis (disorder) — canonical concept |
| ICD-10-CM `A40.*` | Streptococcal sepsis (all subtypes) |
| ICD-10-CM `A41.0–A41.4` | Sepsis due to Staph, H. influenzae, anaerobes |

### 📝 Notes
- `_include=Condition:subject` fetches the associated **Patient** resources in the same bundle.

|---|---|

|---|---|
| `2519-2` | Lactate \[Moles/volume\] in Blood |

```
Patient IDs = UNION(
  Condition results   → .subject.reference,
  MedicationRequest   → .subject.reference,
  Observation         → .subject.reference
)
```

|---|---|---|

- **Comma-separated values** in the `code` parameter apply **OR logic** per the FHIR spec — a single query efficiently matches any of the three codes without multiple round-trips.

```
# Covers IV formulations used in empirical sepsis; also captures MRSA skin/bone/OPAT use

# Covers IV/IM empirical coverage; also captures CAP, meningitis, gonorrhea use
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2193&status=active&intent=order&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_include=MedicationRequest:encounter

# Query 3 — Combined OR cohort (both agents in one pass)
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|11124,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2193&status=active&intent=order&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_include=MedicationRequest:encounter

# Query 4 — Combination therapy cohort: patients on BOTH agents concurrently
# Step A: retrieve patient IDs on vancomycin
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|11124&status=active&intent=order

# Step B: for each patient from Step A, confirm concurrent ceftriaxone order
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2193&status=active&intent=order&subject={patientId}

# Query 5 — Historical completed courses (retrospective cohort)
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|11124,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2193&status=completed&intent=order&authoredon=ge2026-01-01&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

## Query Design Notes

### RxNorm Code Selection

| Agent | RxNorm Ingredient Code | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Vancomycin | `11124` | Ingredient-level; captures all IV/PO formulations (HCl salt codes roll up) |
| Ceftriaxone | `2193` | Ingredient-level; captures all concentrations and routes |

> Using **ingredient-level** codes (vs. product/clinical drug codes) is intentional — it ensures no branded or concentration-specific variant is missed in the cohort sweep.

---

### Parameter Choices

| Parameter | Value | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `status` | `active` | Restricts to currently active therapy (in-progress empirical treatment) |
| `intent` | `order` | Excludes proposals/plans; targets verified provider orders |
| `authoredon` | date range | Enables time-windowed retrospective pulls (Query 5) |
| `_include` | `patient`, `encounter` | Pulls linked Patient and Encounter resources for context enrichment |

---

### ⚠️ Known Cross-Indication Noise (Clinical De-duplication Needed Downstream)

| Drug | Non-Sepsis Indications That Will Appear |
|---|---|
| **Vancomycin** | MRSA cellulitis, osteomyelitis, C. diff colitis (PO), endocarditis, OPAT |
| **Ceftriaxone** | Community-acquired pneumonia, bacterial meningitis, gonorrhea, Lyme disease, pyelonephritis |

**Downstream refinement strategies:**
- Join to **Condition** (`/Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|11612004`) for a sepsis/bacteremia diagnosis on the same encounter
- Join to **Encounter** with `type` or `class` = inpatient ICU to restrict to high-acuity settings
- Cross-reference **Observation** (lactate ≥ 2 mmol/L, procalcitonin elevation, blood culture orders) as objective sepsis markers
- Use **MedicationAdministration** instead of MedicationRequest if your FHIR server tracks actual infusion events (stronger evidence of inpatient empirical use)
